summary: This building material has a high embodied carbon footprint of 4700 kg CO2e per m², largely driven by the hardcore sub-base in the flooring which accounts for over 70% of the total. Architects and builders should prioritize exploring lower-carbon alternatives for the hardcore layer to significantly reduce the building's environmental impact, as well as options for roofing slate alternatives.
